reforms.description: The Pro-active Labour Market Intervention Fund (PALMIF) was established to support innovative active labour market policies during the transition period, providing funding for local programs aimed at addressing specific employment challenges. Additionally, the Technos programme supports R&D projects of innovative enterprises through loans and grants, accessible to any firm in the Czech Republic.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These funding initiatives are designed to enhance R&D capabilities and support innovative projects, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Both funding initiatives are currently operational and providing support.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms provide significant support for R&D funding but do not represent a structural overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: PALMIF was created in 1992 under the auspices of the European Union’s PHARE programme to support government labour market policy during the transition.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic has established a network of EU Innovation Relay Centres to provide technology-transfer services to firms and encourage participation in Community research and technology development programmes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative facilitates the transfer of technology and knowledge from research to the market, enhancing innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The network of Innovation Relay Centres is operational and providing services.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these centres represents a significant effort to improve technology transfer but does not constitute a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The Czech Republic participates in a network of 62 EU Innovation Relay Centres through the FEMIRC-CZ, an innovation centre in Prague which provides technology-transfer services to firms.

reforms.description: The Park programme provides support for the establishment and maintenance of Science and Technology parks that function as business incubators and disseminators of technology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This programme enhances the infrastructure necessary for innovation and technology dissemination, fostering a conducive environment for R&D and commercialization.
reforms.status_evidence: The Park programme is currently operational and supporting technology parks.
reforms.importance_rationale: The support for Science and Technology parks is significant but does not represent a fundamental change in the research infrastructure landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Park, which provides support to the establishment and maintenance of Science and Technology parks that function as business incubators and disseminators of technology.
